project.title: COAD Project
project.summary: An educational research website for exploring COAD PanCanAtlas data, tumor/normal modeling, protein mutation sites, and ChEMBL compound context.
project.researchNotice: For education and research only. Not for medical diagnosis.
background: COAD means colon adenocarcinoma, a type of colon cancer that starts in gland-like cells of the colon.
Colon cancer data analysis matters because public biomedical datasets can reveal patterns in genes, mutations, and sample labels.
Public biomedical data means research data shared by scientific projects so students and researchers can study disease patterns responsibly.
project.audience: Students, teachers, and reviewers who want to understand the project without opening local databases or notebooks.

datasetSummary.label: Clinical
datasetSummary.title: Clinical data
datasetSummary.description: Patient and disease information used to understand sample context.
datasetSummary.plainDefinition: Clinical data means patient and disease information, such as diagnosis or sample type.
datasetSummary.note: Shown as a project summary only, not as a raw patient table.

datasetSummary.label: RNA expression
datasetSummary.title: RNA expression
datasetSummary.description: Gene activity measurements used to build the tumor vs normal feature matrix.
datasetSummary.plainDefinition: RNA expression means how active genes are in a sample.
datasetSummary.note: The TCGA-only adjacent-normal model uses 471 primary tumor samples and 41 solid tissue normal samples.

datasetSummary.label: Mutation
datasetSummary.title: Mutation data
datasetSummary.description: DNA sequence changes found in COAD tumor samples, used for mutation hotspots and protein-viewer context.
datasetSummary.plainDefinition: Mutation data means DNA sequence changes found in tumor samples.
datasetSummary.note: Top mutated genes include APC, TP53, KRAS, and PIK3CA in the project mutation summary.

sections.label: Clinical
sections.title: Clinical data
sections.summary: Patient and disease information used to describe COAD (colon adenocarcinoma, a type of colon cancer) survival patterns.
sections.plainDefinition: Clinical data means patient and disease information, such as age, sex, cancer stage, and survival time.

metrics.label: Total patients
metrics.note: COAD patients in the clinical report.

metrics.label: Stage I-IV
metrics.note: Patients with usable cancer stage labels.

metrics.label: Death events
metrics.note: Observed deaths in this follow-up dataset.

charts.title: Age distribution
charts.takeaway: Most patients are older adults, with the largest groups in 60-69 and 70-79.

charts.title: COAD patient age distribution
charts.takeaway: This Jupyter figure shows the full age distribution chart used in the clinical report.
charts.imageAlt: COAD patient age distribution chart from the clinical survival notebook.

charts.title: Stage distribution
charts.takeaway: Stage II and Stage III are the largest groups in this clinical summary.

charts.title: Gender split
charts.takeaway: The gender split is close, with slightly more male patients.

charts.title: Survival by stage
charts.takeaway: Later stages have lower survival percentages in this report.

charts.title: Overall survival by simplified pathologic stage
charts.takeaway: This Jupyter figure shows that later simplified pathologic stages have lower overall survival in this report.
charts.imageAlt: Overall survival by simplified pathologic stage from the clinical survival notebook.

charts.title: Factors related to prognosis
charts.takeaway: Stage relates more strongly to prognosis than gender in this analysis. Hazard ratio means a number comparing risk between groups.

sections.label: RNA expression
sections.title: RNA expression
sections.summary: Gene activity measurements comparing COAD tumor samples with normal colon-related samples.
sections.plainDefinition: RNA expression means how active genes are in a sample. Higher expression means the gene is producing more RNA signal.

metrics.label: Genes
metrics.note: Genes analyzed after matching the expression data.

charts.title: Tumor vs normal samples
charts.takeaway: The expression analysis compares 449 tumor samples with 308 normal colon-related samples.

charts.title: Differential expression categories
charts.takeaway: Differential expression means checking which genes are more or less active in tumor samples.

charts.title: Top upregulated genes
charts.takeaway: Positive log2 fold change means higher expression in tumor samples.

charts.title: Top downregulated genes
charts.takeaway: Negative log2 fold change means lower expression in tumor samples than in normal samples.

charts.title: Volcano plot
charts.takeaway: A volcano plot shows both effect size and statistical evidence for many genes.
charts.imageAlt: Volcano plot from the COAD gene expression analysis notebook.

charts.title: Heatmap of top differentially expressed genes
charts.takeaway: A heatmap uses color to show expression patterns across samples and genes.
charts.imageAlt: Heatmap from the COAD gene expression analysis notebook.

sections.label: Mutation
sections.title: Mutation data
sections.summary: DNA sequence changes found in COAD tumor samples, including variant types, hotspot positions, and frequently mutated genes.
sections.plainDefinition: Mutation data means DNA sequence changes found in tumor samples. A hotspot is a mutation position that appears repeatedly.

metrics.label: Clinical patients
metrics.note: Patients in the linked clinical summary.

metrics.label: Mutation records
metrics.note: Total mutation records in the report.

metrics.label: Mutated genes
metrics.note: Genes with at least one mutation record.

charts.title: Variant types
charts.takeaway: SNPs are the most common variant type in this mutation dataset.

charts.title: Functional classes
charts.takeaway: Missense mutations change one amino acid in a protein; silent mutations do not change the amino acid.

charts.title: Most frequently mutated genes
charts.takeaway: A sample can have mutations in more than one gene, so these percentages should not be added together.

charts.title: Mutation hotspots
charts.takeaway: A hotspot is a mutation position that appears repeatedly in different tumor samples.

charts.title: Tumor mutation burden summary
charts.takeaway: A few samples have very high mutation counts, so the mean is much higher than the median.

charts.title: Co-mutation heatmap
charts.takeaway: Co-mutation means two genes are mutated in the same sample.
charts.imageAlt: Co-mutation heatmap from the COAD mutation notebook.

charts.title: Mutation frequency by AJCC stage
charts.takeaway: This checks whether common mutation genes appear at different rates across cancer stages.
charts.imageAlt: Mutation frequency by AJCC stage chart from the COAD mutation notebook.

workflowSteps.name: Select COAD samples
workflowSteps.short: Choose COAD tumor and normal samples from curated TCGA/PanCanAtlas-related data.
workflowSteps.details: The website focuses on COAD only, not a pan-cancer comparison.

workflowSteps.name: Clean labels
workflowSteps.short: Standardize sample metadata and labels such as tumor or normal.
workflowSteps.details: Cleaning labels reduces confusion from ambiguous or low-quality entries.

workflowSteps.name: Build feature matrix
workflowSteps.short: Create a table where each row is a sample and each column is a measurable feature.
workflowSteps.details: In this project, many features are gene expression measurements.

workflowSteps.name: Train model
workflowSteps.short: Train machine learning models to separate tumor-like and normal-like samples.
workflowSteps.details: Machine learning means training a program to find patterns from data.

workflowSteps.name: Evaluate metrics
workflowSteps.short: Measure performance with accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and ROC-AUC.
workflowSteps.details: The held-out test split is used only after training to check performance.

workflowSteps.name: Interpret features
workflowSteps.short: Review important genes or molecular features used by the model.
workflowSteps.details: Important features are predictive signals, not proof that a gene causes cancer.

model.inputFeatures: COAD gene expression features from TCGA-only tumor and adjacent-normal samples.
model.trainingSummary: The TCGA-only adjacent-normal report compares Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Linear SVM models using 471 primary tumor samples and 41 solid tissue normal samples.
limitations: This is exploratory research and should not be used for clinical diagnosis.
The adjacent normal class has only 41 samples, so one normal-sample mistake can change the score a lot.
Batch effects are technical differences caused by data being produced in different experiments or pipelines.

metrics.plainEnglish: A simple linear classification model. It performed strongly, but missed 6 tumor samples in the held-out split.
A tree-based model that performed perfectly on this test split. This is promising, but it is still not clinical proof.
A linear model that tries to draw a separating boundary between tumor and normal samples.

proteins.projectReason: KRAS is a frequently mutated COAD gene and affects cell-growth signaling.
proteins.structureNote: AlphaFold DB provides a predicted reference structure. Hotspot locations are shown as educational context.

proteins.projectReason: BRAF p.V600E is a well-known cancer hotspot and appears in the COAD hotspot summary.
proteins.structureNote: The AlphaFold confidence near this residue can be lower than for compact protein regions.

proteins.projectReason: PIK3CA is a common COAD mutation gene involved in growth signaling pathways.
proteins.structureNote: The selected hotspot is shown to connect mutation counts with protein-level biology.

compounds.sourceNote: Common colorectal cancer chemotherapy; shown for formula context only.
Platinum-based chemotherapy used in colorectal cancer regimens.
Topoisomerase I inhibitor used in colorectal cancer regimens.
Multi-kinase inhibitor connected to colorectal cancer treatment research.
BRAF inhibitor relevant to BRAF-mutated colorectal cancer research.
BRAF inhibitor shown as pathway-related context, not as a treatment recommendation.

glossary.definition: Short for colon adenocarcinoma, a type of colon cancer studied in this project.
glossary.projectContext: Project overview, datasets, visualizations, and analyses.

glossary.definition: A cancer that starts in gland-like cells of the colon.
The Cancer Genome Atlas, a public cancer research project that collected molecular and clinical data.
A TCGA project that organized molecular data across many cancer types.
glossary.projectContext: The website uses only the COAD-related part.

glossary.definition: A public database of bioactive molecules, drug-like compounds, and activity data.
A system and database that provide predicted protein structures.
A public cancer genomics portal with tools such as MutationMapper.
A DNA segment that can provide instructions for making RNA or protein.
A measurement of how active genes are in a sample.
A DNA sequence change found in a sample.
A molecule made from amino acids that performs jobs inside cells.
The 3D shape of a protein, which can affect how it works.
A short notation showing the number and type of atoms in a molecule.
A biological sample taken from tumor tissue.
A comparison tissue sample labeled as normal or adjacent normal.
Patient and disease information used to describe sample context.
An organized system for storing and finding data.
A named group of database tables and views.
Data arranged in rows and columns.
A route that lets the frontend request data from the backend.
The part of the website visitors see and interact with.
glossary.projectContext: This website's HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

glossary.definition: The server part of a website that sends data or pages.
A table where rows are samples and columns are measurable features used by a model.
Training a program to find patterns from data.
A machine learning task that predicts which group an item belongs to.
The process where a model learns patterns from training data.
Checking how well a trained model performs on held-out data.
The fraction of all predictions that are correct.
Among samples predicted as a class, the fraction that truly belong to that class.
Among true samples of a class, the fraction the model correctly found.
A combined score that balances precision and recall.
A score that measures how well a model separates classes across thresholds.
Visitors can view data and results but cannot change project records or files.
